quality_highlights: errors.md: excellent lifecycle organization (Schema/Compilation/Runtime/Engine/File/Safe Output); Typo Detection callout box with fuzzy-match note is highly useful; each error section follows a consistent pattern: error code → explanation → code fix example

security-review.md messages: clean, professional, consistent voice; run-started/success/failure use simple declarative statements without exclamation marks; emoji usage is minimal and purposeful (🔍/🔒/⚠️ map to scan/secure/warn)
engine_validation.go: error messages all include example YAML snippets and docs URLs; auth strategy validation covers all edge cases with actionable guidance per strategy type

single_file_improvements.issue: Line 215: The section heading 'Top User-Facing Errors' is misleading — it implies a frequency ranking (these are the top errors) but ALL errors in the document are user-facing. The section actually contains strict mode, MCP configuration, toolset, and permission errors that don't fit the lifecycle-based categories above. Enterprise users scanning for a specific error section will struggle to predict what content is in this section. Rename to 'Strict Mode and Configuration Errors' and update the intro sentence.

Lines 32-34: run-started, run-success, and run-failure messages use an informal consumer-app tone that is inconsistent with the professional register of comparable workflows (e.g., security-review.md). 'Breaking Change Checker online!' is too casual; 'Compatibility verdict delivered! 📋' uses multiple exclamation marks and emoji for what is a neutral success state; the failure message uses the analysis emoji (🔬) instead of a warning indicator.
